titles.ar: بَابُ طِينَةِ الْمُؤْمِنِ وَ الْكَافِرِ
titles.en: Chapter 1 – The clay of the Momin (Believer) and the disbeliever (Kafir)

translations.bn: নিশ্চয়ই আল্লাহ আজ্জা ওয়া জাল্লা নবীগণকে ইল্লিয়্যীনের মাটি থেকে সৃষ্টি করেছেন — তাঁদের হৃদয় ও তাঁদের দেহ — এবং মু'মিনদের হৃদয় সেই একই মাটি থেকে সৃষ্টি করেছেন, এবং মু'মিনদের দেহের সৃষ্টি তার চেয়ে নিম্নতর কিছু থেকে করেছেন। এবং কাফিরদের সিজ্জীনের মাটি থেকে সৃষ্টি করেছেন — তাদের হৃদয় ও তাদের দেহ।
translations.de: Wahrlich, Allah, der Mächtige und Erhabene, erschuf die Propheten aus dem Ton von 'Illiyyin — ihre Herzen und ihre Körper — und erschuf die Herzen der Gläubigen aus demselben Ton und machte die Erschaffung der Körper der Gläubigen aus etwas Geringerem als jenem. Und Er erschuf die Ungläubigen aus dem Ton von Sijjin — ihre Herzen und ihre Körper.
translations.en: Indeed, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, created the prophets from the clay of 'Illiyyin — their hearts and their bodies — and created the hearts of the believers from that same clay, and made the creation of the bodies of the believers from something lesser than that. And He created the disbelievers from the clay of Sijjin — their hearts and their bodies.
translations.es: Ciertamente, Allah, Poderoso y Majestuoso, creó a los profetas de la arcilla de 'Illiyyin — sus corazones y sus cuerpos — y creó los corazones de los creyentes de esa misma arcilla, e hizo la creación de los cuerpos de los creyentes de algo inferior a eso. Y creó a los incrédulos de la arcilla de Sijjin — sus corazones y sus cuerpos.
translations.fa: همانا خداوند عزّ و جلّ پیامبران را از سرشت علّیّین آفرید — دل‌هایشان و بدن‌هایشان را — و دل‌های مؤمنان را از همان سرشت آفرید، و آفرینش بدن‌های مؤمنان را از چیزی فروتر از آن قرار داد. و کافران را از سرشت سجّین آفرید — دل‌هایشان و بدن‌هایشان را.
translations.fr: Certes, Allah, Puissant et Majestueux, a créé les prophètes de l'argile de 'Illiyyîn — leurs cœurs et leurs corps — et a créé les cœurs des croyants de cette même argile, et a fait la création des corps des croyants d'une matière inférieure à cela. Et Il a créé les mécréants de l'argile de Sijjîn — leurs cœurs et leurs corps.
translations.id: Sesungguhnya Allah Azza wa Jalla menciptakan para nabi dari tanah liat 'Illiyyin — hati dan tubuh mereka — dan menciptakan hati orang-orang beriman dari tanah liat yang sama itu, dan menjadikan penciptaan tubuh orang-orang beriman dari sesuatu yang lebih rendah dari itu. Dan Dia menciptakan orang-orang kafir dari tanah liat Sijjin — hati dan tubuh mereka.
translations.ru: Воистину, Аллах, Могущественный и Величественный, создал пророков из глины Иллиййин — их сердца и их тела — и создал сердца верующих из той же глины, а создание тел верующих сделал из чего-то более низкого. И создал неверующих из глины Сиджджин — их сердца и их тела.
translations.tr: Şüphesiz ki Allah Azze ve Celle, peygamberleri İlliyyîn çamurundan yarattı — kalplerini ve bedenlerini — ve müminlerin kalplerini de aynı çamurdan yarattı ve müminlerin bedenlerinin yaratılışını bunun altından kıldı. Kâfirleri ise Siccîn çamurundan yarattı — kalplerini ve bedenlerini.
translations.ur: بے شک اللہ عزّ و جلّ نے انبیاء علیہم السلام کو علّیّین کی مٹی سے پیدا کیا — ان کے دل اور ان کے بدن — اور مومنین کے دلوں کو اسی مٹی سے پیدا کیا، اور مومنین کے بدنوں کی تخلیق اس سے کم تر مٹی سے کی۔ اور کفّار کو سجّین کی مٹی سے پیدا کیا — ان کے دل اور ان کے بدن۔
translations.zh: 确实，安拉——尊贵而威严的——从伊利因的泥土中创造了众先知——他们的心灵和身体——并从那同一泥土中创造了信士们的心灵，而使信士们身体的创造来自于低于此的材质。祂从西吉因的泥土中创造了不信者——他们的心灵和身体。

translations.bn: অতঃপর তিনি দুই মাটি মিশ্রিত করেছেন। তাই এ কারণে মু'মিন কাফিরের জন্ম দেয় এবং কাফির মু'মিনের জন্ম দেয়। এবং এখান থেকে মু'মিন পাপ করে এবং এখান থেকে কাফির সৎকর্ম করে।
translations.de: Dann vermischte Er die beiden Tonarten. Daher zeugt der Gläubige den Ungläubigen und der Ungläubige den Gläubigen. Und daher begeht der Gläubige das Böse und daher tut der Ungläubige das Gute.
translations.en: Then He mixed between the two clays. Thus from this, the believer begets the disbeliever, and the disbeliever begets the believer. And from this, the believer commits evil, and from this, the disbeliever does good.
translations.es: Luego mezcló las dos arcillas. Así, por esto el creyente engendra al incrédulo y el incrédulo engendra al creyente. Y por esto el creyente comete el mal y por esto el incrédulo hace el bien.
translations.fa: سپس میان دو سرشت را مخلوط کرد. پس از همین رو مؤمن کافر می‌زاید و کافر مؤمن می‌زاید. و از همین جا مؤمن مرتکب بدی می‌شود و از همین جا کافر کار نیک انجام می‌دهد.
translations.fr: Puis Il a mélangé les deux argiles. Ainsi, c'est pour cela que le croyant engendre le mécréant et le mécréant engendre le croyant. Et c'est de là que le croyant commet le mal et que le mécréant fait le bien.
translations.id: Kemudian Dia mencampur kedua tanah liat itu. Maka dari itulah orang beriman melahirkan orang kafir, dan orang kafir melahirkan orang beriman. Dan dari sinilah orang beriman melakukan keburukan, dan dari sinilah orang kafir melakukan kebaikan.
translations.ru: Затем Он смешал обе глины. Поэтому верующий рождает неверующего, а неверующий рождает верующего. И отсюда верующий совершает дурное, и отсюда неверующий совершает благое.
translations.tr: Sonra iki çamuru birbirine karıştırdı. İşte bu yüzden mümin kâfiri, kâfir de mümini doğurur. Ve bu yüzden mümin kötülük yapar, kâfir de iyilik yapar.
translations.ur: پھر دونوں مٹیوں کو آپس میں ملا دیا۔ پس اسی وجہ سے مومن کافر کو جنم دیتا ہے اور کافر مومن کو جنم دیتا ہے۔ اور اسی وجہ سے مومن بُرائی کرتا ہے اور اسی وجہ سے کافر نیکی کرتا ہے۔
translations.zh: 然后祂将两种泥土混合。因此，信士生出不信者，不信者生出信士。由此，信士犯了恶行，由此，不信者做了善行。

translations.bn: তাই মু'মিনদের হৃদয় সেই দিকে আকাঙ্ক্ষা করে যা থেকে তাদের সৃষ্টি করা হয়েছে, এবং কাফিরদের হৃদয় সেই দিকে আকাঙ্ক্ষা করে যা থেকে তাদের সৃষ্টি করা হয়েছে।
translations.de: So sehnen sich die Herzen der Gläubigen nach dem, woraus sie erschaffen wurden, und die Herzen der Ungläubigen sehnen sich nach dem, woraus sie erschaffen wurden.
translations.en: So the hearts of the believers yearn toward that from which they were created, and the hearts of the disbelievers yearn toward that from which they were created.
translations.es: Así, los corazones de los creyentes anhelan aquello de lo que fueron creados, y los corazones de los incrédulos anhelan aquello de lo que fueron creados.
translations.fa: پس دل‌های مؤمنان مشتاق آن چیزی هستند که از آن آفریده شده‌اند، و دل‌های کافران مشتاق آن چیزی هستند که از آن آفریده شده‌اند.
translations.fr: Ainsi les cœurs des croyants aspirent vers ce dont ils ont été créés, et les cœurs des mécréants aspirent vers ce dont ils ont été créés.
translations.id: Maka hati orang-orang beriman merindukan apa yang mereka diciptakan darinya, dan hati orang-orang kafir merindukan apa yang mereka diciptakan darinya.
translations.ru: И сердца верующих тоскуют по тому, из чего они были сотворены, и сердца неверующих тоскуют по тому, из чего они были сотворены.
translations.tr: Müminlerin kalpleri yaratıldıkları şeye özlem duyar, kâfirlerin kalpleri de yaratıldıkları şeye özlem duyar.
translations.ur: پس مومنین کے دل اس چیز کی طرف تڑپتے ہیں جس سے وہ پیدا کیے گئے، اور کافروں کے دل اس چیز کی طرف تڑپتے ہیں جس سے وہ پیدا کیے گئے۔
translations.zh: 信士们的心灵向往他们被造之源，不信者们的心灵也向往他们被造之源。

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ رِبْعِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ خَلَقَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِنْ طِينَةِ عِلِّيِّينَ قُلُوبَهُمْ وَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ تِلْكَ الطِّينَةِ وَ جَعَلَ خَلْقَ أَبْدَانِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ وَ خَلَقَ الْكُفَّارَ مِنْ طِينَةِ سِجِّينٍ قُلُوبَهُمْ وَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ فَخَلَطَ بَيْنَ الطِّينَتَيْنِ فَمِنْ هَذَا يَلِدُ الْمُؤْمِنُ الْكَافِرَ وَ يَلِدُ الْكَافِرُ الْمُؤْمِنَ وَ مِنْ هَاهُنَا يُصِيبُ الْمُؤْمِنُ السَّيِّئَةَ وَ مِنْ هَاهُنَا يُصِيبُ الْكَافِرُ الْحَسَنَةَ فَقُلُوبُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ تَحِنُّ إِلَى مَا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ وَ قُلُوبُ الْكَافِرِينَ تَحِنُّ إِلَى مَا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ

narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Ibrahim al-Qummi (d. ~329 AH), prominent Shia exegete and hadith scholar

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, father of Ali ibn Ibrahim. Identification is well established in hadith sciences.
narrators.known_identity: Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi (d. ~early 4th century AH), a major narrator in Shia hadith
Hammad ibn Isa al-Juhani (d. 209 AH), a companion of Imam al-Sadiq and Imam al-Kadhim (AS)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Most likely Rib'i ibn Abdullah ibn al-Jarud al-Basri, a reliable narrator from the companions of Imam al-Sadiq (AS).
An unnamed narrator in the chain. This introduces a degree of disconnection (irsal) in the chain, as the identity of this person is unknown.

narrators.known_identity: Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn Zayn al-Abidin (AS), the fourth Imam (d. 95 AH)

bn.تحنّ: আকাঙ্ক্ষা করা — প্রতিটি হৃদয়ের তার আদি সৃষ্টির উৎসের প্রতি স্বাভাবিক টান
bn.طينة سجّين: সিজ্জীনের মাটি — সেই নিম্ন উপাদান যা থেকে কাফিররা সৃষ্ট; কুরআন ৮৩:৭-৮-এ উল্লেখিত
bn.طينة علّيّين: ইল্লিয়্যীনের মাটি — সেই মহান উপাদান যা থেকে নবী ও মু'মিনদের হৃদয় সৃষ্ট; কুরআন ৮৩:১৮-১৯-এ উল্লেখিত

de.تحنّ: Sich sehnen — beschreibt die angeborene Anziehung jedes Herzens zu seinem ursprünglichen Ursprung
de.طينة سجّين: Ton von Sijjin — die niedere Substanz, aus der die Ungläubigen erschaffen wurden; erwähnt im Koran 83:7-8
de.طينة علّيّين: Ton von 'Illiyyin — die erhabene Substanz, aus der die Propheten und die Herzen der Gläubigen erschaffen wurden; erwähnt im Koran 83:18-19

en.تحنّ: To yearn or long for — describes the innate pull of each heart toward its primordial origin
en.طينة سجّين: Sijjin clay — the base substance from which disbelievers were created; Sijjin is referenced in Quran 83:7-8 as a register of the wicked in the lowest place
en.طينة علّيّين: 'Illiyyin clay — the exalted substance from which prophets and believers' hearts were created; 'Illiyyin is referenced in Quran 83:18-19 as a register of the righteous in the highest heaven

es.تحنّ: Anhelar — describe la atracción innata de cada corazón hacia su origen primordial
es.طينة سجّين: Arcilla de Sijjin — la sustancia baja de la que se crearon los incrédulos; referida en el Corán 83:7-8
es.طينة علّيّين: Arcilla de 'Illiyyin — la sustancia exaltada de la que se crearon los profetas y los corazones de los creyentes; referida en el Corán 83:18-19

fa.تحنّ: اشتیاق — گرایش فطری هر دل به سوی اصل و مبدأ خود
fa.طينة سجّين: سرشت سجّین — مادّه پست که کافران از آن آفریده شدند؛ در قرآن ۸۳:۷-۸ آمده
fa.طينة علّيّين: سرشت علّیّین — مادّه والایی که پیامبران و دل‌های مؤمنان از آن آفریده شدند؛ در قرآن ۸۳:۱۸-۱۹ آمده

fr.تحنّ: Aspirer — décrit l'attirance innée de chaque cœur vers son origine primordiale
fr.طينة سجّين: Argile de Sijjîn — la substance basse dont furent créés les mécréants ; mentionnée dans le Coran 83:7-8
fr.طينة علّيّين: Argile de 'Illiyyîn — la substance exaltée dont furent créés les prophètes et les cœurs des croyants ; mentionnée dans le Coran 83:18-19

id.تحنّ: Merindukan — tarikan alami setiap hati menuju asal penciptaannya
id.طينة سجّين: Tanah liat Sijjin — substansi rendah dari mana orang kafir diciptakan; disebutkan dalam Al-Quran 83:7-8
id.طينة علّيّين: Tanah liat 'Illiyyin — substansi mulia dari mana para nabi dan hati orang beriman diciptakan; disebutkan dalam Al-Quran 83:18-19

ru.تحنّ: Тосковать — описывает врождённое влечение каждого сердца к своему изначальному истоку
ru.طينة سجّين: Глина Сиджджин — низменная субстанция, из которой были созданы неверующие; упоминается в Коране 83:7-8
ru.طينة علّيّين: Глина Иллиййин — возвышенная субстанция, из которой были созданы пророки и сердца верующих; упоминается в Коране 83:18-19

tr.تحنّ: Özlem duymak — her kalbin asıl yaratılışına duyduğu doğal çekim
tr.طينة سجّين: Siccîn çamuru — kâfirlerin yaratıldığı aşağı madde; Kur'an 83:7-8'de zikredilir
tr.طينة علّيّين: İlliyyîn çamuru — peygamberlerin ve müminlerin kalplerinin yaratıldığı yüce madde; Kur'an 83:18-19'da zikredilir

ur.تحنّ: تڑپنا — ہر دل کا اپنی اصل مٹی کی طرف فطری میلان
ur.طينة سجّين: سجّین کی مٹی — وہ پست مادہ جس سے کفّار پیدا کیے گئے
ur.طينة علّيّين: علّیّین کی مٹی — وہ بلند مادہ جس سے انبیاء اور مومنین کے دل پیدا کیے گئے

zh.تحنّ: 向往——描述每颗心灵对其原初来源的天然渴望
zh.طينة سجّين: 西吉因之土——创造不信者的卑下物质；见《古兰经》83:7-8
zh.طينة علّيّين: 伊利因之土——创造先知和信士心灵的崇高物质；见《古兰经》83:18-19

seo_questions.bn: ইসলামে মু'মিনের সন্তান কাফির এবং কাফিরের সন্তান মু'মিন হতে পারে কেন?
seo_questions.de: Warum kann im Islam ein Gläubiger ein ungläubiges Kind haben und umgekehrt?
seo_questions.en: Why does Islam teach that a believer can have a disbelieving child and vice versa?
seo_questions.es: ¿Por qué en el Islam un creyente puede tener un hijo incrédulo y viceversa?
seo_questions.fa: چرا در اسلام ممکن است فرزند مؤمن کافر و فرزند کافر مؤمن باشد؟
seo_questions.fr: Pourquoi en Islam un croyant peut-il avoir un enfant mécréant et inversement ?
seo_questions.id: Mengapa dalam Islam orang beriman bisa memiliki anak kafir dan sebaliknya?
seo_questions.ru: Почему в исламе у верующего может родиться неверующий ребёнок и наоборот?
seo_questions.tr: İslam'da müminin çocuğu kâfir, kâfirin çocuğu mümin olabilir mi?
seo_questions.ur: اسلام میں مومن کے بچے کافر اور کافر کے بچے مومن کیوں ہو سکتے ہیں؟
seo_questions.zh: 为什么伊斯兰教认为信士的孩子可能成为不信者，反之亦然？

similar_content_hints.description: Other hadiths in al-Kafi Volume 2, Book 1 (Book of Faith and Disbelief) that discuss the primordial nature (tinah) of believers and disbelievers, including how God kneaded the clays and the spiritual implications of their mixing.
Hadiths about the creation of human beings from different types of clay or soil, including the hadith that Adam was created from all types of earth, explaining the diversity of his descendants.
Quranic verses about 'Illiyyin (83:18-21) and Sijjin (83:7-9), describing the registers of the righteous and the wicked, which provide the scriptural basis for this hadith's cosmological framework.

summaries.bn: ইমাম যায়নুল আবিদীন (আ.) মানুষের আত্মা ও দেহের সৃষ্টিতত্ত্ব ব্যাখ্যা করেছেন। নবীগণ সম্পূর্ণভাবে ইল্লিয়্যীনের মাটি থেকে, মু'মিনদের হৃদয় একই মাটি থেকে কিন্তু তাদের দেহ নিম্নতর উপাদান থেকে, এবং কাফিররা সিজ্জীনের মাটি থেকে সৃষ্ট। আল্লাহ দুই মাটি মিশ্রিত করেছেন, তাই মু'মিন থেকে কাফির এবং কাফির থেকে মু'মিন জন্ম নেয়, আর প্রতিটি হৃদয় তার আদি উৎসের প্রতি আকৃষ্ট হয়।
summaries.de: Imam Zayn al-Abidin (F) erklärt den kosmologischen Ursprung der menschlichen Seelen und Körper. Die Propheten wurden vollständig aus dem Ton von 'Illiyyin erschaffen, die Herzen der Gläubigen aus demselben Ton, aber ihre Körper aus geringerem Material, und die Ungläubigen aus dem Ton von Sijjin. Gott vermischte die beiden Tonarten, weshalb ein Gläubiger einen Ungläubigen zeugen kann und umgekehrt, und jedes Herz sich nach seiner ursprünglichen Substanz sehnt.
summaries.en: Imam Zayn al-Abidin (AS) explains the cosmological origin of human souls and bodies. The prophets were created entirely from the exalted clay of 'Illiyyin, believers' hearts share that same clay though their bodies are from lesser material, and disbelievers were created from the base clay of Sijjin. God then intermixed these clays, which explains why a believing parent may have a disbelieving child and vice versa, and why believers sometimes sin while disbelievers sometimes do good — yet each heart ultimately yearns toward its original substance. This teaching was transmitted in a scholarly circle addressing questions about the nature of faith and disbelief, providing the early Shia community with a metaphysical framework for understanding moral complexity.
summaries.es: El Imam Zayn al-Abidin (P) explica el origen cosmológico de las almas y los cuerpos humanos. Los profetas fueron creados enteramente de la arcilla de 'Illiyyin, los corazones de los creyentes de la misma arcilla pero sus cuerpos de un material inferior, y los incrédulos de la arcilla de Sijjin. Dios mezcló ambas arcillas, lo que explica por qué un creyente puede engendrar un incrédulo y viceversa, y por qué cada corazón anhela su sustancia original.
summaries.fa: امام زین العابدین علیه السلام خاستگاه کیهانی ارواح و بدن‌های انسان‌ها را تبیین می‌فرمایند. پیامبران از سرشت علّیّین، دل‌های مؤمنان از همان سرشت ولی بدنشان از مادّه‌ای فروتر، و کافران از سرشت سجّین آفریده شدند. خداوند دو سرشت را آمیخت و از همین رو مؤمن از کافر و کافر از مؤمن زاده می‌شود و هر دل به سوی اصل خود می‌گراید.
summaries.fr: L'Imam Zayn al-Abidîn (P) explique l'origine cosmologique des âmes et des corps humains. Les prophètes furent créés entièrement de l'argile de 'Illiyyîn, les cœurs des croyants de la même argile mais leurs corps d'une matière inférieure, et les mécréants de l'argile de Sijjîn. Dieu mélangea les deux argiles, ce qui explique pourquoi un croyant peut engendrer un mécréant et inversement, et pourquoi chaque cœur aspire à sa substance originelle.
summaries.id: Imam Zainal Abidin (a.s.) menjelaskan asal-usul kosmologis jiwa dan tubuh manusia. Para nabi diciptakan sepenuhnya dari tanah liat 'Illiyyin, hati orang beriman dari tanah yang sama namun tubuh mereka dari bahan yang lebih rendah, dan orang kafir dari tanah Sijjin. Allah mencampur kedua tanah tersebut sehingga orang beriman bisa melahirkan orang kafir dan sebaliknya, dan setiap hati rindu pada asal penciptaannya.
summaries.ru: Имам Зайн аль-Абидин (мир ему) объясняет космологическое происхождение человеческих душ и тел. Пророки были полностью созданы из глины Иллиййин, сердца верующих — из той же глины, но их тела — из менее возвышенного материала, а неверующие — из глины Сиджджин. Аллах смешал обе глины, поэтому верующий может породить неверующего и наоборот, а каждое сердце тоскует по своей первоначальной субстанции.
summaries.tr: İmam Zeynelâbidîn (a.s.), insanların ruhlarının ve bedenlerinin kozmolojik kökenini açıklamaktadır. Peygamberler tamamen İlliyyîn çamurundan, müminlerin kalpleri aynı çamurdan fakat bedenleri daha aşağı bir maddeden, kâfirler ise Siccîn çamurundan yaratılmıştır. Allah iki çamuru karıştırmış, böylece mümin kâfiri doğurabilir, kâfir mümini doğurabilir ve her kalp asıl mayasına özlem duyar.
summaries.ur: امام زین العابدین علیہ السلام نے انسانی روحوں اور بدنوں کی تخلیق کا بیان فرمایا۔ انبیاء علّیّین کی مٹی سے بنائے گئے، مومنین کے دل اسی مٹی سے ہیں لیکن ان کے بدن اس سے کم تر ہیں، اور کفّار سجّین کی مٹی سے بنائے گئے۔ اللہ نے دونوں مٹیوں کو ملایا، اسی لیے مومن سے کافر اور کافر سے مومن پیدا ہوتا ہے، اور ہر دل اپنی اصل کی طرف کھنچتا ہے۔
summaries.zh: 伊玛目宰因·阿比丁（愿主赐他平安）解释了人类灵魂和身体的宇宙起源。众先知完全由伊利因之土创造，信士的心灵同出于此土但身体来自较低材质，不信者则由西吉因之土创造。安拉混合了两种泥土，因此信士可能生出不信者，反之亦然，每颗心灵都向往其原初本质。

translation.de: so sehnen sich die Herzen der

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/ 7)
text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ خَلَقَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِنْ طِينَةِ عِلِّيِّينَ قُلُوبَهُمْ وَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ تِلْكَ الطِّينَةِ وَ جَعَلَ خَلْقَ أَبْدَانِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ وَ خَلَقَ الْكُفَّارَ مِنْ طِينَةِ سِجِّينٍ قُلُوبَهُمْ وَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ

فَخَلَطَ بَيْنَ الطِّينَتَيْنِ فَمِنْ هَذَا يَلِدُ الْمُؤْمِنُ الْكَافِرَ وَ يَلِدُ الْكَافِرُ الْمُؤْمِنَ وَ مِنْ هَاهُنَا يُصِيبُ الْمُؤْمِنُ السَّيِّئَةَ وَ مِنْ هَاهُنَا يُصِيبُ الْكَافِرُ الْحَسَنَةَ فَقُلُوبُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ تَحِنُّ إِلَى مَا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ وَ قُلُوبُ الْكَافِرِينَ تَحِنُّ إِلَى مَا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Rabie Bin Abdullah, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Aliasws Bin Al-Husaynasws having said: ‘Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Created the Prophetsas from the clay of Illiyeen, theiras hearts and theiras bodies, and Created the hearts of the Momineen (plural of Momin) from that very clay, and Made the creation of the bodies of the Momineen (plural of Momin) to be from besides that; and Heazwj Created the disbelivers from the clay of Sijjeen - their hearts as well as their bodies.

Then Heazwj Blended between the two clays. Thus, from this the Believer begets the disbeliever, and the disbeliever begets the Believer, and from over here the Believer commits the evil and from over here the disbeliever does good deeds. So the hearts of the Momineen (plural of Momin) yearn towards what they had been Created from, and the hearts of the disbelievers yearn towards what they have been Created from’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Rib’i ibn ‘Abd Allah from a man from Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) who has said the following: “Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, created the prophets from the clay of ‘Illiyiyyn (paradise), both their bodies and souls. He made the hearts of the believers from such clay and He made their bodies from a world lower than that world. He created the unbelievers, both their bodies and souls from the clay of Sijjin (fire). He thus mixed the two clays. For this reason children of unbelievers become believers and vice versa. For this reason the believers get afflicted with evil and unbelievers receive good things. Thus, the hearts of the believers incline to that from which they are created and the hearts of the unbelievers incline to that from which they are created.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/ 7)
text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ خَلَقَ الْمُؤْمِنَ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْجَنَّةِ وَ خَلَقَ الْكَافِرَ مِنْ طِينَةِ النَّارِ

وَ قَالَ إِذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِعَبْدٍ خَيْراً طَيَّبَ رُوحَهُ وَ جَسَدَهُ فَلَا يَسْمَعُ شَيْئاً مِنَ الْخَيْرِ إِلَّا عَرَفَهُ وَ لَا يَسْمَعُ شَيْئاً مِنَ الْمُنْكَرِ إِلَّا أَنْكَرَهُ

قَالَ وَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ الطِّينَاتُ ثَلَاثٌ طِينَةُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَ الْمُؤْمِنُ مِنْ تِلْكَ الطِّينَةِ إِلَّا أَنَّ الْأَنْبِيَاءَ هُمْ مِنْ صَفْوَتِهَا هُمُ الْأَصْلُ وَ لَهُمْ فَضْلُهُمْ وَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الْفَرْعُ مِنْ طِينٍ لَازِبٍ كَذَلِكَ لَا يُفَرِّقُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بَيْنَهُمْ وَ بَيْنَ شِيعَتِهِمْ
وَ قَالَ طِينَةُ النَّاصِبِ مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُونٍ وَ أَمَّا الْمُسْتَضْعَفُونَ فَمِنْ تُرَابٍ لَا يَتَحَوَّلُ مُؤْمِنٌ عَنْ إِيمَانِهِ وَ لَا نَاصِبٌ عَنْ نَصْبِهِ وَ لِلَّهِ الْمَشِيئَةُ فِيهِمْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Al Nazar Bin Shuayb, from Abdul Ghaffar Al Jaazy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Created the Believer from the clay of the Paradise, and Created the disbeliever from the clay of the Fire’.

And he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘And when Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Intends goodness with a servant, Cleans his soul and his body, so he does not hear anything from the goodness except that he recognises it, and he does not hear anything from the evil except that he rejects it’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘And I heard himasws saying: ‘The clays are three (types) – the clay of the Prophetsasws, and the Believer is from that very clay except that the Prophetsas, theyas are from its elite. Theyas are the roots, and for themas is theiras merit, and the Momineen (plural of Momin) are the branches from the sticky clay, thus Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic will not Separate between themas and theiras Shia’.

And heasws said: ‘The clay of the Hostile One (Nasibi) is from [15:26] from black mud Fashioned into shape; and as for the weak ones (of understanding), so (they are) from dust. Neither does the Believer change over from his Emān (belief), nor does the Hostile One (Nasibi) change over from his hostility, and for Allahazwj, there is the Volition regarding them’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan from al- Nadr ibn Shu’ayb from ‘Abd al-Ghaffar al-Jaziyy from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, created the believers from the clay of paradise. He created the unbelievers from the clay of fire. He then said, ‘When Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, decides to grant good to one of His servants. He purifies his spirit and body. Whenever he hears any good thing he recognizes it. Whenever he hears any bad things he dislikes them.’ I (the narrator) then heard him saying, ‘The clays are of three kinds: (a) one is the clay of the prophets from which the believers are also created, except that the prophets are made of the superior kind of clay. The prophets are the roots and the believers are the branches and for this reason they are more excellent than the believers. The believers are created from a stickier kind of clay. Thus Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious will not keep them apart from their followers.’ The Imam than said, ‘The clay of those who insult (‘ A’immah ) are from a black clay that smells foul. The weak ones are made from soil. The believers do not change their faith nor do evil doers (those who insult ‘ A’immah , recipients of divine supreme covenant) do so and Allah has a definite goal behind it.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/ 7)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ مِنْ أَيِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ طِينَةَ الْمُؤْمِنِ فَقَالَ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ فَلَمْ تَنْجَسْ أَبَداً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Salih Bin Sahl who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! From which thing did Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Create the clay of the Believer?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘From the clay of the Prophets<sup>as</sup>, so they would never be unclean, ever!’
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn Mahbub from Salih ibn Sahl who has said that I asked abu abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) the following: “May Allah keep my soul of service to you, from what kind of material has Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, created the clay of the believers? He then replied, ‘He has created them with the clay from which the prophets were made. Thus, they never become unclean.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/ 7)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ جَلَّ وَ عَزَّ خَلَقَنَا مِنْ أَعْلَى عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَ شِيعَتِنَا مِمَّا خَلَقَنَا مِنْهُ وَ خَلَقَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ وَ قُلُوبُهُمْ تَهْوِي إِلَيْنَا لِأَنَّهَا خُلِقَتْ مِمَّا خُلِقْنَا مِنْهُ
ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتابَ الْأَبْرارِ لَفِي عِلِّيِّينَ وَ ما أَدْراكَ ما عِلِّيُّونَ كِتابٌ مَرْقُومٌ يَشْهَدُهُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ

وَ خَلَقَ عَدُوَّنَا مِنْ سِجِّينٍ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَ شِيعَتِهِمْ مِمَّا خَلَقَهُمْ مِنْهُ وَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ فَقُلُوبُهُمْ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ لِأَنَّهَا خُلِقَتْ مِمَّا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتابَ الفُجَّارِ لَفِي سِجِّينٍ وَ ما أَدْراكَ ما سِجِّينٌ كِتابٌ مَرْقُومٌ وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, and someone else, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, and someone else, from Muhammad Bin Khalaf, from Abu Nahshal who said, ‘Muhammad Bin Ismail narrated to me, from Abu Hamza Al Sumaly who said,

‘I heard Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> saying that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Majestic and Mighty created us<sup>asws</sup> from the high Illiyeen and Created the hearts of our<sup>asws</sup> Shia from what He<sup>azwj</sup> Created us<sup>asws</sup> from, and Created their bodies from besides that, and their hearts lend to yearn towards us<sup>asws</sup>, because these have been Created from what we<sup>asws</sup> have been Created from’.

Then heasws recited these Verses [83:18] Nay! Most surely the record of the righteous shall be in the Iliyeen. [83:19] And what will make you know what the highest Iliyeen is? [83:20] It is a written book, [83:21] Those of Proximity witness it.

And Heazwj Created ourasws enemies from Sijjeen, and Created their adherents from what Heazwj Created them from, and their bodies from besides that. Thus, their hearts yearn towards them because these were Created from what they were Created from’. Then heasws recited these Verses [83:7] Nay! most surely the record of the wicked is in the Sijjeen. [83:8] And what will make you know what the Sijjeen is? [83:9] It is a written book. [83:10] Woe on that day to the beliers’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya and others have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalaf from abu Nahshal who has said that Muhammad ibn ‘Isma’il narrated from abu Hamza al-Thumali who has the following: “I heard abu la’ far (a.s.) saying, ‘Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, created us from the top most of paradise. He made the hearts of our followers from that which He had created us. He created their bodies from that which is below, thus, their hearts incline to us; they are made from what He had made us.’ He then recited this verse of the Holy Quran. ‘However, the records of the deeds of the virtuous ones will certainly be in llliyin [83:18]. Would that you had known what llliyin is! (19). It is a comprehensively written Book (of records) (20). The ones nearest to Allah will bring it to the public [83:21].’ He then said, ‘Allah has created our enemies from Sijjin (the fire) and He has created the hearts of their followers from what He had created them. He created the bodies of their followers (followers of our enemies) from other matters. Thus, their hearts (the hearts of the followers of our enemies) incline to them (to our enemies) because they are created from what they (our enemies) are created.’ Then he recited this verse of the Holy Quran: ‘Let them know that the records of the sinners’ deeds are in Sijin [83:7]. Would that you had known what Sijin is! (8). It is a comprehensively written Book (of records) (9). Woe, on that day, to those who have rejected Allah’s revelations (10) and those who have rejected the Day of Judgment [83:11].’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/ 7)
text: أَخْبَرَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ كَيْسَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ أَنَا مَوْلَاكَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ كَيْسَانَ قَالَ أَمَّا النَّسَبُ فَأَعْرِفُهُ وَ أَمَّا أَنْتَ فَلَسْتُ أَعْرِفُكَ قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنِّي وُلِدْتُ بِالْجَبَلِ وَ نَشَأْتُ فِي أَرْضِ فَارِسَ وَ إِنَّنِي أُخَالِطُ النَّاسَ فِي التِّجَارَاتِ وَ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ فَأُخَالِطُ الرَّجُلَ فَأَرَى لَهُ حُسْنَ السَّمْتِ وَ حُسْنَ الْخُلُقِ وَ كَثْرَةَ أَمَانَةٍ ثُمَّ أُفَتِّشُهُ فَأَتَبَيَّنُهُ عَنْ عَدَاوَتِكُمْ وَ أُخَالِطُ الرَّجُلَ فَأَرَى مِنْهُ سُوءَ الْخُلُقِ وَ قِلَّةَ أَمَانَةٍ وَ زَعَارَّةً ثُمَّ أُفَتِّشُهُ فَأَتَبَيَّنُهُ عَنْ وَلَايَتِكُمْ فَكَيْفَ يَكُونُ ذَلِكَ

فَقَالَ لِي أَ مَا عَلِمْتَ يَا ابْنَ كَيْسَانَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَخَذَ طِينَةً مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ وَ طِينَةً مِنَ النَّارِ فَخَلَطَهُمَا جَمِيعاً ثُمَّ نَزَعَ هَذِهِ مِنْ هَذِهِ وَ هَذِهِ مِنْ هَذِهِ فَمَا رَأَيْتَ مِنْ أُولَئِكَ مِنَ الْأَمَانَةِ وَ حُسْنِ الْخُلُقِ وَ حُسْنِ السَّمْتِ فَمِمَّا مَسَّتْهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْجَنَّةِ وَ هُمْ يَعُودُونَ إِلَى مَا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ وَ مَا رَأَيْتَ مِنْ هَؤُلَاءِ مِنْ قِلَّةِ الْأَمَانَةِ وَ سُوءِ الْخُلُقِ وَ الزَّعَارَّةِ فَمِمَّا مَسَّتْهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةِ النَّارِ وَ هُمْ يَعُودُونَ إِلَى مَا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, and someone else, from Al Husayn Bin Al Hassan, altogether from Muhammad Bin Awrama, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Ismail Bin Yasaar, from Usman Bin Yusuf who said, ‘Abdullah Bin Kaysan infromed me,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, ‘I am your<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> friend Abdullah Bin Kaysan’. He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘As for the lineage, so I<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> recognise it, and as for you, so I<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> do not recognise you’. I said to him<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, ‘I was born in the mountains and grew up in the land of Persia, and I tend to blend in with the people regarding business and other than that. So I came across the man and I saw him as a good listener and good manners and a lot of trustworthiness. When I investigate I find him to be one of your<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> enemies; and I came across the man and saw evil manners from him, and as being of little trustworthiness, and meanness. When I investigate I find him to be one of your<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> friends. So how did that happen?’

So he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said to me: ‘But, do you not know, O Ibn Kaysan, that Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Took a clay from the Paradise and a clay from the Fire. So He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Blended the two together. Then He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Removed this from this, and this from this. Thus, what you see from those as being the ones of trustworthiness, and goodly mannerisms, and as good listeners, so it is from what has touched them from the clay of the Paradise, and they would be returning to what they had been Created from; and what you see from these as being of the ones with little trustworthiness, and evil mannerisms, and the meanness, so it is from what touched them from the clay of the Fire, and they would be returning to what they had been Created from’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad, who has narrated from more than one person from al-Husayn ibn al-Hassan. All of the above have narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Uwarma from Muhammad ibn Ali from ‘Isma’il ibn Yasar from ‘Uthman ibn Yusuf who has said that, reported to him ‘Abd Allah ibn Kaysan, from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Once I said to the Imam, ‘May Allah keep my soul in service for your cause, I am your servant, abu ‘Abd Allah ibn Kaysan.’ The Imam said, ‘I know your genealogy. However, I do not know you (so well).’ I said, ‘I was born in the mountains and grew up in the land of Persia. I associate with people in business and other occasions. I, thus, associate with a man and find his appearance very good, to be of good manners and more trustworthy. When I investigate 1 find him to be one of your enemies. I also associate with another man who has bad manners, is less trustworthy and hot tempered. I then investigate and find him to be of your supporters and followers. Why is it so?’ “He (the Imam) said, ‘O ibn Kaysan, Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, took a clay from paradise and also from fire and then mixed them together. Then He separated this from that and that from this. The good appearances, good manners and trustworthiness that you find in them (our enemies) is because of the touch of the clay from paradise but they will return to that from which they are made. The deceitfulness, bad manners and hot-temperament that you find in them (our supporters and followers) is because of the touch of the clay from fire, however, they will return to that from which they were made.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (10/ 7)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْمُؤْمِنُونَ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Salih Bin Sahl who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘The Momineen (plural of Momin) are from the clay of the Prophetsas?’ Heasws said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn Khalid from Salih ibn Sahl who has said the following: “I asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Are the believers made from the clay from which the prophets were made?’ He said, ‘Yes, that is correct.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/10)

parts.text: الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ

text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَمَّا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَخْلُقَ آدَمَ ( عليه السلام ) بَعَثَ جَبْرَئِيلَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي أَوَّلِ سَاعَةٍ مِنْ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ فَقَبَضَ بِيَمِينِهِ قَبْضَةً بَلَغَتْ قَبْضَتُهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ السَّابِعَةِ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ الدُّنْيَا وَ أَخَذَ مِنْ كُلِّ سَمَاءٍ تُرْبَةً وَ قَبَضَ قَبْضَةً أُخْرَى مِنَ الْأَرْضِ السَّابِعَةِ الْعُلْيَا إِلَى الْأَرْضِ السَّابِعَةِ الْقُصْوَى

فَأَمَرَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ كَلِمَتَهُ فَأَمْسَكَ الْقَبْضَةَ الْأُولَى بِيَمِينِهِ وَ الْقَبْضَةَ الْأُخْرَى بِشِمَالِهِ فَفَلَقَ الطِّينَ فِلْقَتَيْنِ فَذَرَا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ ذَرْواً وَ مِنَ السَّمَاوَاتِ ذَرْواً فَقَالَ لِلَّذِي بِيَمِينِهِ مِنْكَ الرُّسُلُ وَ الْأَنْبِيَاءُ وَ الْأَوْصِيَاءُ وَ الصِّدِّيقُونَ وَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَ السُّعَدَاءُ وَ مَنْ أُرِيدُ كَرَامَتَهُ فَوَجَبَ لَهُمْ مَا قَالَ كَمَا قَالَ
وَ قَالَ لِلَّذِي بِشِمَالِهِ مِنْكَ الْجَبَّارُونَ وَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ وَ الْكَافِرُونَ وَ الطَّوَاغِيتُ وَ مَنْ أُرِيدُ هَوَانَهُ وَ شِقْوَتَهُ فَوَجَبَ لَهُمْ مَا قَالَ كَمَا قَالَ

ثُمَّ إِنَّ الطِّينَتَيْنِ خُلِطَتَا جَمِيعاً وَ ذَلِكَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِنَّ اللَّهَ فالِقُ الْحَبِّ وَ النَّوى فَالْحَبُّ طِينَةُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الَّتِي أَلْقَى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهَا مَحَبَّتَهُ وَ النَّوَى طِينَةُ الْكَافِرِينَ الَّذِينَ نَأَوْا عَنْ كُلِّ خَيْرٍ وَ إِنَّمَا سُمِّيَ النَّوَى مِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّهُ نَأَى عَنْ كُلِّ خَيْرٍ وَ تَبَاعَدَ عَنْهُ

وَ قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَ مُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتِ مِنَ الْحَيِّ فَالْحَيُّ الْمُؤْمِنُ الَّذِي تَخْرُجُ طِينَتُهُ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْكَافِرِ وَ الْمَيِّتُ الَّذِي يَخْرُجُ مِنَ الْحَيِّ هُوَ الْكَافِرُ الَّذِي يَخْرُجُ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْمُؤْمِنِ فَالْحَيُّ الْمُؤْمِنُ وَ الْمَيِّتُ الْكَافِرُ وَ ذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَ وَ مَنْ كانَ مَيْتاً فَأَحْيَيْناهُ فَكَانَ مَوْتُهُ اخْتِلَاطَ طِينَتِهِ مَعَ طِينَةِ الْكَافِرِ وَ كَانَ حَيَاتُهُ حِينَ فَرَّقَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بَيْنَهُمَا بِكَلِمَتِهِ

كَذَلِكَ يُخْرِجُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ فِي الْمِيلَادِ مِنَ الظُّلْمَةِ بَعْدَ دُخُولِهِ فِيهَا إِلَى النُّورِ وَ يُخْرِجُ الْكَافِرَ مِنَ النُّورِ إِلَى الظُّلْمَةِ بَعْدَ دُخُولِهِ إِلَى النُّورِ وَ ذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لِيُنْذِرَ مَنْ كانَ حَيًّا وَ يَحِقَّ الْقَوْلُ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Salih Bin Abu Hammad, from Al Husayn Bin Yazeed, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Ibrahim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, when He<sup>azwj</sup> Intended to Create Adam<sup>as</sup>, Sent Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> during the beginning time from the day of Friday. So he<sup>as</sup> grabbed a handful in his<sup>as</sup> right hand, his<sup>as</sup> handful reaching from the seventh sky to the sky of the world; and he<sup>as</sup> took some dust from every sky. And he<sup>as</sup> grabbed another handful from the highest seventh earth (firmament) to the furthest seventh earth (firmament).

Then, Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Commanded Hisazwj Word (Kalimat-Allahazwj) so it withheld the former handful in his right hand and the latter handful in his left hand, and split the clay into two parts and scattered from the earth with a scattering, and from the skies with a scattering. So Heazwj Said to those in his right hand: “From you would be the Rasoolsas, and the Prophetsas, and the successorsas, and the truthful ones, and the Momineen (plural of Momin), and the fortunate ones, and the ones who want its prestige”. Thus, it Obligated upon them what Heazwj Said, just as Heazwj Said it to be.

And He<sup>azwj</sup> Said to those in its left hand: “From you would be the tyrants, and the Polytheists, and the disbelievers, and the despots, and the ones who wants its indignity and its wickedness”. Thus, it Obligated for them what He<sup>azwj</sup> Said just as He<sup>azwj</sup> Said it to be.

Then the two clays were both mixed together, and these are the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [6:95] Surely, Allah is the Splitter of the seed and the stone. So the ‘seed’ is the clay of the Momineen (plural of Momin) upon which Allahazwj Cast Hisazwj Love; and the ‘stone’ is the clay of the disbelievers which is distanced from every goodness. And rather, it is named as the ‘stone’ from the reason that it is away from every goodness and remote from it.

And Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Said [6:95] He Extracts the living from the dead and He is the Extractor of the dead from the living. So the ‘living’ is the Believer whose clay is extracted from the clay of the disbeliever; and the ‘dead’ who is extracted from the living, he is the disbeliever who comes out from the clay of the Believer. So the ‘living’ is the Believer, and the ‘dead’ is the disbeliever, and these are the Words of the Mighty and Majestic [6:122] Is he who was dead then We Raised him to life. So his death was the mixture of his clay along with the clay of the disbeliever, and his life was when Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Separated between the two by Hisazwj Word (Kalimat-Allahazwj).

Like that Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Extracts the Believer during the birth from the darkness after his entry into it - to the light, and Heazwj Extracts the disbeliever from the light towards the darkness after his entry into the light; and these are the Words of the Mighty and Majestic [36:70] That it may warn him who is alive, and (that) the Word may prove true against the unbelievers’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Salih ibn abu Hammad from al-Husayn ibn Yazid from al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn abu Hamza from Ibrahim from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “When Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, willed to create Adam He sent Jibril in the first hours of the Friday. He picked up with his right hand a handful. His hand had in its reach from the seventh heavens to the heaven above earth. From each of the heavens he picked up a handful. He also picked up another handful from the seventh earth of the above-most and to the farthest seventh earth. Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, then commanded His word to hold the first handful in his right and the other in his left. The clay then was cleaved into two. It was scattered from earth and from the heavens and then said to the clay in his right hand, ‘From you will be the messengers, the prophets, their successors, the truthful ones, the believers, those who will achieve salvation and those whom I will to have honors.’ It then became necessary for them to be as He said them to be. He then said to that which was on his left, ‘From you will be the tyrants, the polytheists, the unbelievers, the devils and those whom I will to humiliate and be of the wicked ones.’ It then became necessary for them to become what He said them to become. Thereafter the two clays mixed together as Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, has said, ‘Allah makes the seed and the nuts to cleave apart.’ [6:95] The seed is the clay of the believers upon which Allah has placed His love. The nuts are the clay of the unbelievers that are far from all good. It is called nuts (al- Nawa’) because it is far away from all good and from Him. Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, has said, ‘He (Allah) brings forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living.’ [6:95] The living are the believers who come out from the unbelievers and the dead who come out of the living are the unbelievers who are from the believers. The living are the believers and the dead are the unbelievers. This is because of the words of Allah, ‘Are those whom We brought to life after their being dead . . .’ [6:122] Their death was the mixing of their clay with the clay of the unbelievers and his coming to life was when Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, caused separation between them through His word. In this way Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, takes the believers at birth out to light from darkness after their entering therein and He takes the unbelievers out of the light to darkness after their entering into the light as it is said in the words of Allah, the Majestic the Glorious, ‘So that he can give warning to those who are of the living and the word about the unbelievers truly come to pass.’” [36:70]
